summary:
Stress breakers are often avoided due to their potential to cause structural weakening around abutments by applying inappropriate forces; they also pose risks for damaging surrounding bone tissue such as the alveolar ridge when used improperly during procedures involving dental implants.
The avoidance stems from documented adverse effects related primarily to weakened support elements near implanted sites and risk factors associated directly impacting jawbone integrity post-procedure. Sentence count matches requirement exactly at two while capturing essential aspects pertinent to addressing why this tool has fallen out favor within contemporary practices concerning oral health maintenance techniques utilizing advanced materials and methods aimed specifically towards minimizing invasive impacts wherever possible without compromising efficacy outcomes necessary for long-term success rates achievable today compared historically speaking based upon evolving scientific understanding regarding biological responses vis-a-vis mechanical interactions occurring between human anatomy versus artificial constructs introduced therapeutically into mouths requiring precise control over applied loads/distances etc...
